comments.author: reader
comments.text: Here's a very important that I forgot to bring up earlier. The time-shifts apply within Elm too. Suppose I play my own game autonomously with 4 moves. When I go to play the instant replay, the instant replay needs to animate it differently then when I first executed it. It still needs to be faithful to time intervals WITHIN the drag. But it doesn't use the original intervals BETWEEN drags.  It's a re-telling of the original story that is faithful only to the elements of the original action that were relevant in real time.

comments.author: reader
comments.text: Use 80ms/pixel for now (which is probably exaggerated slownness), but we will conduct easy experiments to measure this soon.
